description: Adapt LiberoYoumuDataset for actual LIBERO dataset format (episode-per-file, JSONL metadata, flat column names), verify exact parity with LeRobot, and run e2e training

userStories.title: Adapt metadata loading for JSONL format
userStories.description: As a developer, I need load_episode_metadata to load episode metadata from meta/episodes.jsonl (JSONL format) since the actual LIBERO dataset does not have meta/episodes/chunk-000/file-000.parquet. The function should auto-detect which format is present.
acceptanceCriteria: New function load_episode_metadata_jsonl in libero_utils.py reads meta/episodes.jsonl and returns list[EpisodeDescriptor]
For episode-per-file layout, file_index equals episode_index (each file = one episode)
start_frame and end_frame computed by accumulating episode lengths sorted by episode_index
Works for multi-chunk datasets (chunk-000 has episodes 0-999, chunk-001 has 1000-1692)
Update load_episode_metadata to auto-detect: try parquet path first, fall back to JSONL
Unit test verifies correct EpisodeDescriptor values for a small JSONL fixture
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: JSONL format: {"episode_index": 0, "tasks": ["..."], "length": 214} per line. No data/file_index or dataset_from_index fields — these must be derived.

userStories.title: Adapt LiberoYoumuDataset for episode-per-file layout and flat column names
userStories.description: As a developer, I need LiberoYoumuDataset to work with the actual LIBERO dataset where each episode is a separate file (episode_XXXXXX.parquet) with flat column names (state, actions, image) instead of the LeRobot layout (file-NNN.parquet with dotted names like observation.state).
acceptanceCriteria: _load_chunk auto-detects file naming: episode_XXXXXX.parquet vs file-NNN.parquet
For episode-per-file layout, episode-to-file mapping is direct: episode N maps to episode_{N:06d}.parquet with row_offset=0
Constructor defaults updated: state_column='state', action_column='actions', image_column='image'
Output dict keys remain 'observation.state', 'action', 'observation.images.image', 'episode_index', 'frame_index' regardless of underlying column names
Image struct extraction still works: image column is struct<bytes: binary, path: string>, extract bytes child
Unit test: construct dataset on real data at /mnt/local/localcache00/libero_64KB, call __getitem__(0) and verify output shapes and dtypes
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Dataset schema: image: struct<bytes,path>, wrist_image: struct<bytes,path>, state: fixed_size_list<float>[8], actions: fixed_size_list<float>[7], timestamp: float, frame_index: int64, episode_index: int64, index: int64, task_index: int64. 1693 episodes across 2 chunks (1000 + 693). Each file has ~3 row groups with ~100 rows.

userStories.title: Adapt task description loading for JSONL metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I need load_libero_task_descriptions (in veomni/data/multimodal/data_transform.py) to load task descriptions from meta/episodes.jsonl since the actual dataset uses JSONL, not parquet episode metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: load_libero_task_descriptions accepts a path to episodes.jsonl and reads episode_index + tasks[0] per line
Returns dict[int, str] mapping episode_index to task description string
Falls back to 'perform the task' if tasks field is missing or empty
Training script (train_qwen_vl_libero.py) metadata path resolution updated: checks for meta/episodes.jsonl in addition to existing parquet candidates
Unit test verifies correct loading from a small JSONL fixture
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current code in train_qwen_vl_libero.py checks meta/episodes/episodes.parquet and meta/episodes/chunk-000/file-000.parquet — neither exists. Must add meta/episodes.jsonl as a candidate.

userStories.title: Exact numerical parity test (youmu vs LeRobot)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pytest test that proves LiberoYoumuDataset returns exactly the same data as LeRobot's dataset for the same sample indices, giving confidence that training with youmu is correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at tests/test_youmu_lerobot_parity.py (or similar location in the test directory)
Test loads the same dataset with both youmu and lerobot backends via build_libero_dataset from tasks/omni/train_qwen_vl_libero.py

For indices [0, 1, 2, 7, 8, 9, len-3, len-2, len-1] plus 5 random indices, asserts: observation.state tensors exactly equal (torch.equal), action tensors exactly equal, observation.images.image tensors exactly equal (pixel-perfect), episode_index values match
Test passes on the 64KB parquet dataset at /mnt/local/localcache00/libero_64KB (youmu side) and /mnt/local/localcache00/libero (lerobot side if needed)
Test skips gracefully if dataset directory or lerobot package is unavailable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: LeRobot may need the original dataset path if it has different parquet expectations. The youmu side should use the 64KB dataset. Both should produce identical outputs for matching indices.

userStories.title: Update training config and run e2e training
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update the training YAML config to point to the 64KB dataset and verify the full training pipeline runs at least 1 step successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: configs/multimodal/qwen3_vl/qwen3_vl_libero.yaml libero_data_dir set to /mnt/local/localcache00/libero_64KB
bash train.sh tasks/omni/train_qwen_vl_libero.py configs/multimodal/qwen3_vl/qwen3_vl_libero.yaml launches without import or config errors
Training completes at least 1 full step (forward + backward + optimizer step) without crash
Loss logged at step 1 is a finite number (not NaN/inf)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Single-node multi-GPU with FSDP2. Config uses: obs_len=1, pred_len=4, micro_batch_size=1, global_batch_size=64, flash_attention_2, rmpad_with_pos_ids=true. Step 1 loss=4.0726, Step 2 loss=2.3834.
